guide:
What is Core?

Hapana Core (core.hapana.com) is the member management platform used by STRONG Pilates. It handles client records, session scheduling, package and membership management, payments, and reporting across all 142 STRONG locations. After logging in, Core presents an Account Picker — select a location to load that studio's dashboard.

Read-only principle: We never edit, create, or update contacts directly in Core. Core is the source of truth; changes flow one-way into Grow via the Core-to-Grow sync.

Client IDs are base64-encoded. The numeric ID (e.g., 3076262) is encoded as MzA3NjI2Mg== in URLs. You can decode with echo 'MzA3NjI2Mg==' | base64 -d.

Finding a Contact

The Clients page is at /index.php?route=dashboard/clients. There are two search methods and two critical filter traps.

Quick Search Bar

The full-width input at the top of the client table (#clientsearch) searches by Name, Email, Barcode, or Phone. It has a 1500ms debounce — after you stop typing, it fires an AJAX reload of the table. The value gets copied to hidden field #clientnamesearch, then #updatelist (the hidden Filter button) is triggered.

Advanced Filter Bar

Below the search bar, columns can be filtered via a dropdown selector (parent_selection[]) with options: First Name, Last Name, Gender, Birth Month, Age, Email, Phone, Client Status. Match types: contains, begins with, ends with, is.

The Alumni Status Gotcha

The status filter (#filter_status) defaults to Active Clients. A contact who cancelled their membership is an Alumni, not Active. You will not find them unless you switch the filter to Alumni Clients.

Status filter options: Active Clients, Alumni Clients, Removed Clients, Deleted Clients.

Corporate Client LookUp

The Corporate Client LookUp button (#corpClientLookup_btn) toggles to a second DataTable (#clientLookupTable) that searches across all locations in the account. Use this when you know the person exists in Core but don't know which location they're at. Click "Back" to return to the single-location view.

Direct URL (if you have the client ID)

If you already have the base64-encoded client ID, go directly:

/index.php?route=dashboard/clients/assessment&cid={BASE64_CLIENT_ID}

Via API (read-only): GET /v2/customer/client?email={email} or ?phone={phone} or ?barcode={barcode}.

Sessions vs Packages — The "Show Visits" Trick

The most common confusion in Core: the Past Sessions view does NOT show which package paid for each session. It only shows "Paid by credits" without identifying the source package.

The answer is on the Packages section, not the Sessions section.

Every package (active or archived) has two links:

  • "Show Visits" (pkgcreditsbookinghistory) — Shows every session consumed from that specific package with columns: S.N, Booking Date Time, Description (class name + date/time), Location Of Use (Home/Away), Credits Used/Refunded, Status (Attended/Cancelled/Late Cancel/No Show)
  • "View History" (pkgpaymentdetails) — Shows the payment/billing history for that specific package

How to Trace Sessions for a Package

  1. Go to the client detail page
  2. Look at the Active Packages section — or click Archived Packages to see past/expired packages (intro offers, expired class packs, past memberships)
  3. Each package shows: Package Name, Credit Limit, valid for {type} valid until {date}, and credit counts (Available/Booked/Used)
  4. Click "Show Visits" on the target package — this is the definitive list of sessions consumed under that package

For intro offers specifically: The intro offer package will be in Archived Packages if it's expired. Click "Archived Packages" to find it.

For free classes: Look for $0.00 packages (e.g., "Friend Free STRONG Class") in either active or archived. "Show Visits" works the same way.

The session view dropdown (#client-schedules) has 4 options: Booked Sessions, Past Sessions, Cancelled Sessions, Schedule Activity Stream. All require a date range (#schedule_date_from / #schedule_date_to) and clicking the #filer_client_schedule button (note: "filer", not "filter" — this is misspelled in the source). Default range is ~60 days back.

Reports That Look Similar But Need Different Filters

Three groups of near-identical reports that differ by what transactions or statuses they include. Getting these wrong means missing data.

Group A: Revenue Transaction Reports

Three reports, same columns, different scope:

Report Filter Name What It Shows What It Excludes
Net Revenue Detail getNetRevenueDetail Completed/settled transactions only In-processing and failed transactions
Net Revenue + In Processing getAllNetRevenueDetail2 Completed + in-processing transactions Failed transactions only
All Invoices Report getAllTransactionsReport Every invoice created in date range Nothing — shows current status of each

All three share the same columns: S.No, Full Name, Email, Payment Method, Invoice Date, Transaction Category, Description, Transaction Id, Processed By, Sale Referred By, Payment Date, Payment Status, Admin Fee, Failed Payment Fee, Transaction Fee, Tax, Discount, Gross Revenue, Revenue Type, Net Revenue.

Payment Status values: Revenue, Refund, In Processing, Failed, Partially Refunded.

Revenue Type values: Session Package, Membership, No Show Fee, Late Cancel Fee, POS, Gift Card.

When to use which: Use "Net Revenue Detail" for clean revenue numbers. Use "All Invoices" when investigating a specific transaction's current status (a refunded transaction may show as "Revenue" in Net Revenue Detail but "Refund" in All Invoices if refunded after the reporting date). Use "Net Revenue + In Processing" when you need to include pending payments.

Group B: Membership Status Reports

Report Filter Name Default View What You Miss Without Filter Changes
Membership Details getMembershipDetails Active only Pending, Suspended, Cancelled, Complete, Scheduled, Partially Refunded
Client on Memberships getactivemembershippkgs Active only Same — cancelled/suspended members are invisible
Client Not On Membership getnonactivemembershippkgs Not on active membership Clients on suspended/pending memberships appear here even though they technically have one
Cancellation Report cancelledMemberships Cancelled only Active/suspended/pending members

Membership Details is the most complete report. It has a Membership Status filter with checkboxes: Active, Pending, Suspended, Cancelled, Complete, Scheduled, Partially Refunded. You must check all statuses to get the full picture. The default "Active" view misses anyone who cancelled, suspended, or completed their membership.

Membership Details columns: S.No, Full Name, Email, Phone, Address, Postal Code, Member Created Date, Package Name, Package Type, Package Category, Package Price, Package Status, Date Sold, Intro Offer Purchase Date, Auto Renew, Auto Renew Date, Expiry Date, Completed Date, Last Payment Date, Last Payment Amount, Next Payment Date, Next Payment Amount, Member Inactive Date, Cancel Date, Cancellation Reason.

Group C: Payment Failure Reports

Report Filter Name What It Shows
Failed Payments paymentfailed Failed payments still pending (unresolved)
Failed Payment History paymentfailedhistory All failed payments with their current status (may now be resolved)

Same concept as Group A: one shows the current unresolved problem, the other shows the full history including resolved failures.

searchMethods.name: Quick Search
searchMethods.description: Full-width input at the top of the client table. Searches by Name, Email, Barcode, or Phone. Has a 1500ms debounce — after you stop typing, it fires an AJAX reload of the table. The value gets copied to hidden field #clientnamesearch, then #updatelist (the hidden Filter button) is triggered.

searchMethods.name: Advanced Filter Bar
searchMethods.description: Below the search bar, columns can be filtered via a dropdown selector (parent_selection[]) with options: First Name, Last Name, Gender, Birth Month, Age, Email, Phone, Client Status. Match types: contains, begins with, ends with, is.

searchMethods.name: Corporate Client LookUp
searchMethods.description: Toggles to a second DataTable (#clientLookupTable) that searches across ALL locations in the account. Use when you know the person exists in Core but don't know which location they're at. Click Back to return to single-location view.

searchMethods.name: Direct URL
searchMethods.description: If you already have the base64-encoded client ID, go directly to /index.php?route=dashboard/clients/assessment&cid={BASE64_CLIENT_ID}. The numeric ID is base64-encoded (e.g., MzA3NjI2Mg== = 3076262).

mainNav.notes: Retail Dashboard, New Sale, Retail Configurations

accountSwitching.warning: Never load directly in browser — returns plain text 'changed' that bricks the tab's rendering. Always use AJAX (XHR from within a Core page) or via the updateSessionID() JavaScript function on the account picker page.
accountSwitching.resetCookies: Clear siteID, v1Info, PHPSESSID cookies then navigate to https://core.hapana.com/?_=reset
accountSwitching.resetScript: ['siteID','v1Info','PHPSESSID'].forEach(function(n){ document.cookie = n + '=; path=/; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 GMT'; document.cookie = n + '=; path=/; domain=.hapana.com; expires=Thu, 01 Jan 1970 00:00:00 GMT'; });

dashboard.notes: Stats are blurred by default — click 'Show stats' button to reveal.

publicApi.credentialsLocation: ~/00. Claude Projects/05. STRONG Pilates/04. STRONG HQ - Core API/.env

clientApis.description: Lookup by email, phone, or barcode

coreGrowSync.syncNotes: Sync is NOT instant — can be hours. During high-traffic periods (campaigns, openings), longer delays. Manual edits in Grow get overwritten on next sync.

fields.name: First Name
fields.definition: Contact’s first name

fields.name: Last Name
fields.definition: Contact’s last name

fields.name: Email
fields.definition: Contact’s e-mail
fields.observations: The e-mail address is unique across the location

fields.name: Phone
fields.definition: Contact’s phone number
fields.observations: Only syncs validated phone numbers  Phone numbers are unique across the location  Won’t sync the phone field if the conditions above are not satisfied

fields.name: Date Of Birth
fields.definition: Contact’s birthday

fields.name: Contact Source
fields.definition: Reserved to Opportunities

fields.name: Contact Type
fields.definition: Type of the contact
fields.observations: grow-sync service syncs with the fixed value customer  All contacts synced with grow-sync service are existing clients in Core, so if the contact type was lead in Grow before, it will become customer after the service starts syncing it.

fields.name: Street Address
fields.definition: Contact’s street address
fields.observations: May hold the contact’s complete address depending on the location

fields.name: City
fields.definition: Contact’s city

fields.name: State
fields.definition: Contact’s state

fields.name: Country
fields.definition: Contact’s country

fields.name: Postal Code
fields.definition: Contact’s postal code

fields.name: DND Text Messages
fields.definition: Contact’s Do Not Disturb option for SMS
fields.observations: Compliant with Permanent DND Grow settings, not trying to sync permanent opt-outs

fields.name: DND Emails
fields.definition: Contact’s Do Not Disturb option for E-mail
fields.observations: Compliant with Permanent DND Grow settings, not trying to sync permanent opt-outs

fields.name: DND all channels
fields.definition: Contact’s general Do Not Disturb option
fields.observations: Checked when DND Text Messages and DND Emails are marked

fields.name: Core Status
fields.definition: Contact’s status, according to the location
fields.observations: Possible values: Client  Alumni  Removed

fields.name: Past Active Packages
fields.definition: Every package ever registered for the Contact that is not currently active for the location

fields.name: Active Package
fields.definition: Every currently active package from the Contact for the location

fields.name: Active Package Category
fields.definition: Every package category from the contact’s currently active packages for the location

fields.name: Join Date
fields.definition: Date the contact has joined the location

fields.name: First Attendance Date
fields.definition: Contact’s first visit date to the location

fields.name: Intro Offer
fields.definition: Marks if the contact has already claimed the intro offer to the location
fields.observations: Possible values: claimed  pending   Once set to claimed, won't change back

fields.name: Corporate Status
fields.definition: Contact’s status on corporate level
fields.observations: ² Given the scope of the information, this field is updated by the HQ Flow. So there might be a delay in syncing a change, depending on when the HQ flow is scheduled.  Possible values: suspendedMembership  activePackage  introOffer  inactive  accountCreated

fields.name: Anniversary
fields.definition: Number of years since the Join Date
fields.observations: Complete years

fields.name: Days Absent
fields.definition: (Milestone) Number of days since the last session date
fields.observations: 0 if there's a future booking  Possible values: 0, 1, …, 29, 30, 45, 60, 90, 120

fields.name: Days Inactive
fields.definition: (Milestone) Number of days since: The last date since the contact used a credit in the location  If never, the last date since the contact purchased a package in the location  If never, the Join Date
fields.observations: 0 if there's a future booking  Possible values: 0, 1, 7, 15, 30, 45, 60, 90, 180, 365, 730, 1095

fields.name: Late Cancel Accrual
fields.definition: (Milestone) Number of times the contact was absent due to Late Cancel since the last attendance date in the location
fields.observations: Possible values: 0, 1, 2, 3 If there is a future booking, a session that is booked but not attended to, this number will always be 0 even if the user late canceled or was a not show on the last sessions.

fields.name: No Show Accrual
fields.definition: (Milestone) Number of times the contact was absent due to No Show since the last attendance date in the location

fields.name: Late Cancel No Show Accrual
fields.definition: (Milestone) Sum of the fields Late Cancel Accrual and No Show Accrual

fields.name: Home Location
fields.definition: Contact’s home location(s), based on the corresponding active packages.
fields.observations: A home location is any location a contact has active package(s)  Contacts without active packages will keep the home location empty  Suspended packages also count for the home location list  A contact may have more than one home location.

fields.name: Attendance Total
fields.definition: Number of times a contact has checked-in to the location

fields.name: Corporate Attendance
fields.definition: Number of times a contact has checked-in to every location of the brand
fields.observations: ³ Given the scope of the information, this field is updated by the HQ Flow. So there might be a delay in syncing a change, depending on when the HQ flow is scheduled. However, contacts that are new to the location will early update this field once to enable faster workflows on Grow.

fields.name: Location Status
fields.definition: Contact’s status on location level  The logic to determine the Location Status goes in the following order: For that Client, is every active package an intro offer? If yes: introOffer  Does it have any suspended packages? If yes: suspendedMembership  Does it have any active package? If yes: activePackage  Does past packages contain anything? If yes: inactive  If none of the above conditions (in order) are met, then: accountCreated
fields.observations: Possible values: suspendedMembership  activePackage  introOffer  inactive  accountCreated

fields.name: First Booking
fields.definition: Contact’s flag to indicate if any booking was already registered, based on the First Booking Date
fields.observations: Possible values: booked  pending   Once set to booked, won't change back

fields.name: First Booking Status
fields.definition: Contact’s flag to indicate if any booking was already registered Logic: Client never booked a class (Fist Booking Date is null) or the booking still has the status as “Pending” - pending  Client has a First Session Date set - attended  First Booking Date is set and It is not attended, cancelled or no show - booked  First Booking Date is set and It is attended - attended  First Booking Date is set and it’s either No Show or Late Cancel - late cancel no show  First Booking Date is set: it was Cancelled - cancelled  First Attendance is null - cancelled  There is no booking in the future - cancelled   First Booking Date is set: it was Cancelled / No Show - rebooked  First Attendance is null - rebooked  There is a booking in the future - rebooked   First Booking Date is set: it was Cancelled - attended  First Attendance is Set - attended
fields.observations: Possible values: pending  booked  rebooked  attended  late cancel no show  cancelled   To handle eventual delays on the trainer side to set the client’s attendance mark, we consider a tolerance of 8 hours before setting late cancel no show. Still, as soon as the session is marked attended, the values is resolved to attended

fields.name: First Booking Date
fields.definition: Contact’s first booking date in the location
fields.observations: The status of the session doesn’t matter, even if it was cancelled

fields.name: Promo Codes Used
fields.definition: List of every coupon code the contact has ever used in the location

fields.name: Referrals
fields.definition: Number of times the contact was referred by other clients of the same location

fields.name: Core Source
fields.definition: Through which medium the contact met the brand
fields.observations: Lead source from oc_customer.lead_source

fields.name: Email Opt In
fields.definition: Contact’s opt in/out for email
fields.observations: Possible values: Opt In  Opt Out

fields.name: SMS Opt In
fields.definition: Contact’s opt in/out for SMS
fields.observations: Possible values: Opt In  Opt Out

fields.name: Tags
fields.definition: Contact’s tags managed by grow-sync service for the location
fields.observations: Tags not managed by grow-sync service won't be altered by it  See more in the Tags Section

fields.name: List of all tags managed by grow-sync service:
